title: PLA Navy conducts live-fire exercises in Taiwan Strait
body: A carrier group led by the Shandong transited the Taiwan Strait this week, according to statements from U.S. Indo-Pacific Command.

title: DPRK tests Hwasong-18 ICBM, KPA statement says
body: The Korean People's Army announced a successful Hwasong-18 test launch; Japan and South Korea condemned the launch.

title: Kadena F-15s participate in Balikatan with Philippine Air Force
body: Exercise Balikatan kicked off in the Philippines with participation from Kadena-based F-15 squadrons.

title: AUKUS leaders meet to review submarine timeline
body: Officials from Australia, the United Kingdom, and the United States met to discuss the AUKUS submarine program and progress on Pillar Two.

title: Houthi missile strikes commercial vessel in Red Sea
body: A bulk carrier transiting the Bab-el-Mandeb was struck by a Houthi anti-ship missile; U.S. Central Command released imagery of the impact.

title: IRGC commander warns of response to Israeli strike
body: The IRGC Quds Force issued a statement warning of retaliation. Tehran threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Al-Asad airbase hit by indirect fire, no casualties
body: Kata'ib Hezbollah claimed responsibility for a rocket attack on Al Asad Air Base in Iraq.

title: Operation Prosperity Guardian intercepts multiple drones
body: A coalition task force under Combined Maritime Forces intercepted Houthi one-way attack drones over the Red Sea.

title: Russian Iskander strike reported in Kharkiv oblast
body: Ukrainian officials reported an Iskander-M strike near Kharkiv. The VKS has increased sortie rates across the front.

title: NATO ministers approve Baltic air policing rotation from Lakenheath
body: F-35As based at RAF Lakenheath will rotate into Baltic air policing; Article 5 language featured prominently in the ministerial communique.

title: Wagner successor Africa Corps expands footprint in Mali
body: Russia's Africa Corps (the Wagner successor) expanded deployments in Mali, working with the ruling junta.

title: Ramstein format convenes to coordinate Ukraine aid
body: Defense ministers gathered at Ramstein Air Base for the Ukraine Defense Contact Group meeting, with pledges from more than forty nations.

title: Al-Shabaab attack on Somali National Army base repelled
body: A complex Al-Shabaab assault on an SNA forward operating base south of Mogadishu was repelled with AFRICOM airstrike support.

title: Camp Lemonnier hosts combined medical exercise
body: U.S. Africa Command personnel assigned to CJTF-HOA conducted a combined medical exercise at Camp Lemonnier in Djibouti.

title: JNIM expands Sahel reach after withdrawal from Niger
body: Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) expanded operations across the Sahel following the U.S. withdrawal from Air Base 201 near Agadez.

title: JIATF-S tracks go-fast vessel transiting Caribbean
body: Joint Interagency Task Force South coordinated the interdiction of a go-fast vessel in the Caribbean Basin.

title: Tren de Aragua expands operations across Andean Ridge
body: Venezuelan criminal syndicate Tren de Aragua has expanded operations through Colombia and the Andean Ridge.

title: NORAD intercepts Russian Tu-95 in Alaskan ADIZ
body: NORAD fighters intercepted two Russian Tu-95 bombers entering the Alaskan Air Defense Identification Zone during a homeland defense patrol.

title: GSSAP detects on-orbit RPO near GEO
body: U.S. Space Command reported a rendezvous and proximity operation near geostationary orbit, with GSSAP providing space domain awareness coverage.

title: Shijian-21 conducts maneuvers near defunct satellite
body: China's Shijian-21 conducted on-orbit servicing maneuvers, approaching a defunct Beidou satellite. Analysts note counterspace implications.

title: Minuteman III test launch from Vandenberg
body: Air Force Global Strike Command conducted an unarmed Minuteman III ICBM test launch from Vandenberg Space Force Base, with STRATCOM oversight.

title: Russia test-fires RS-28 Sarmat ICBM
body: Russia conducted a test launch of the RS-28 Sarmat heavy ICBM. New START treaty limits are again under discussion.

title: Volt Typhoon pre-positioning in U.S. critical infrastructure
body: JFHQ-DoDIN issued an advisory on Volt Typhoon activity targeting water and energy sector networks.

title: APT28 phishing campaign targets NATO ministries
body: A Sandworm-adjacent APT28 phishing campaign targeted several NATO defense ministries, according to a hunt-forward readout.

title: MARSOC rotations to Pacific partner nations continue
body: Marine Raider teams from MARSOC conducted foreign internal defense training with Philippine counterparts.

title: C-17 crews activate Civil Reserve Air Fleet for surge
body: U.S. Transportation Command activated a segment of the Civil Reserve Air Fleet to augment C-17 capacity for a CENTCOM contingency.

title: Red Sea shipping disruptions ripple to Suez and East Africa
body: Maersk rerouted vessels around the Cape of Good Hope; Suez Canal revenue fell sharply and Horn of Africa ports saw surges.

title: Silicon Valley VC raises new AI fund
body: A venture fund announced a billion-dollar vehicle to invest in generative AI startups. No defense implications were discussed.

title: Bank of Canada holds interest rates steady
body: The Bank of Canada kept its overnight rate unchanged. Inflation eased for the fourth consecutive month.

title: Premier League clubs approve spending cap
body: English Premier League clubs voted in favor of a new squad spending cap to take effect next season.
